LSE-HS10-L4-1400P-HSSCIENG910-ESC-EUR-01 • HS Science Engineering Design 9-10 • Breaking Large Problems Into Sub-Problems

The Cathedral Door Lock

Click   🎧   to listen.
Listen to a conversation between a girl and a boy. Listen carefully. You will hear the conversation only once. After listening, you will answer a question.
Listen to the Conversation
QUESTION:
What is the boy trying to do?
Choose the best answer. You may change your answer before submitting.
SMART SUPPORT:
Go back to the conversation. Construct a complete academic justification. State your claim about why the boy redirects the girl from the full list to a single piece, support that claim with reasoning drawn directly from what he says about stopping and picking one thing, and articulate the conclusion that follows about how a large problem becomes solvable when it is broken into smaller parts.
CA ELD PI.B.5  |  NGSS HS-ETS1-2  |  HS Engineering Design 9-10: Breaking Large Problems Into Sub-Problems
